edge: The abstract mentions that ViTs have achieved success but suffer from vulnerable resolution scalability, where the performance drops drastically with unseen input resolutions during training.
ResFormer is introduced as a framework built upon the idea of multi-resolution training for improved performance on a wide spectrum of testing resolutions.
ResFormer operates on replicated images of different resolutions and enforces a scale consistency loss to engage interactive information across different scales.
A global-local positional embedding strategy is proposed to alternate among varying resolutions effectively, especially novel ones in testing, ensuring smooth changes conditioned on input sizes.
Extensive experiments are conducted for image classification on ImageNet, showing promising scaling abilities of ResFormer towards a wide range of resolutions.

node_2: top-1 accuracy on low and high resolutions
edge: ResFormer-B-MR achieves a Top-1 accuracy of 75.86% and 81.72% on relatively low and high resolutions respectively, which are better than DeiT-B by 48% and 7.49%.

ResFormer framework was motivated by training on a sample of different scales and aided by a scale-consistency loss.
ResFormer introduced a global-local positional embedding strategy to facilitate better generalization on unseen resolutions.

ResFormer can be readily adapted to downstream tasks such as semantic segmentation, object detection, and video action recognition.
